name: Snow Report 2026-03-01 05:55:20
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

Yesterday was a beautiful day to be on the mountain enjoying all the terrain we have to offer. The sun made a few welcomed appearances, and when it’s this nice here, it’s hard to ask for much more. Just good turns, great views, and another memorable day.

A few early snow showers may linger this morning, but skies will gradually turn mostly sunny as the day rolls on. Highs will climb to around 20 degrees at the base and 15 degrees at the peak as they’ll fall throughout the day, with northwest winds coming in at 16 – 18 mph.

The slopes served up packed powder early yesterday, with softer surfaces developing as the day went on. It’s a nice reminder that spring isn’t too far off. The firmer patches here and there will be smoothed out by our grooming team to help keep things consistent. Bumps are skiing well on trails where you can link together a nice, steady line, like on Upper Giant Killer. 

Our grooming crew went over the same plan except for Upper Pike which will be skiing & riding on natural terrain. Groomers remain the go-to for carving, with softer snow allowing you to really sink in and enjoy each turn. If you’re looking for where to go, you can’t go wrong with runs on Ace of Clubs or Gold Rush.  For the full list of groomed trails, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Our main lifts are scheduled for an 8:30 a.m. opening which includes the Golden Express Quad, Summit Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet. The Knomes Knoll Triple is due to spin shortly after at 9:00 a.m. 

Have a great Sunday! 

 – Evan

Period of 7 days/week lift operations: February 16th - March 2nd.   

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.   

Uphill Travel   

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.              

Mountain Services   

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.  

Tickets & Lessons   

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation   

Pico Fitness Center                              

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.      

Hours of Operation:   

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.          

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-28 05:57:01
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

This weekend is shaping up as another ideal time to be here. Today will feel a bit more like spring, Sunday will feel a bit more like winter, but conditions have been great no matter what your preference is. 

Today’s weather will be a bit warm and cloudy, with wind gusts potentially having some effect on lifts in the early morning. Temps will stay comfortable throughout, with highs peaking in the mid-30s at the base, with winds due to die down during the afternoon. 

Our main lifts are scheduled for an 8:30 a.m. opening which includes the Golden Express Quad, Summit Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet. The Knomes Knoll Triple is due to spin shortly after at 9:00 a.m. 

The snow softened up a bit yesterday and will keep a similar feel, even with less sunshine than we saw. Groomers will start out with plenty of give even in the morning, while well-trodden surfaces will likely see some loose granular by the late afternoon. Today brought more fun surfaces in the glades, with Birch Woods as a particular highlight. 

Our groomers tackled many of the regulars while adding in Upper Pike and Poma Loop, giving our upper mountain an extra groomed route down. Lower Sunset 71 and Lower Giant Killer will also be smoothed out for the morning. For the full list of groomed trails, head to our Lifts & Trails Report. 

Hope you can get out there to savor this season that just keeps on giving. 

 – Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-27 05:51:23
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

The clouds revealed a big stretch of blue sky, and the sunshine set the stage for the perfect kind of day here yesterday. Conditions are skiing and riding beautifully from top to bottom, with surfaces feeling smooth and consistent all the way down.

We’re still sitting at 100% open terrain, so there’s plenty of room to roam and find your favorite line. Today will bring mostly sunny skies with highs near 29 at the base and 23 at the top, along with breezy winds of 15 to 20 mph coming from the southwest. 

Yesterday’s snow had that classic feel with perfect packed powder and a little extra grip that really lets you settle into your turns with confidence. The glades are skiing especially well right now. Mid Pike Glades and Birch Woods both have great coverage, and the snow in there is still nice and soft. If you’ve been thinking about dipping into the trees, it’s a great time to explore.

Our grooming team added Knomes Knoll terrain back into the mix with trails like Ace of Clubs and Triple Slope holding fresh corduroy by the time we open. Hit up Fools Gold for some smooth carving in the morning or B Slope for some more speedy turns. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of groomed trails ahead of your day on the hill. 

The Summit Express Quad, Knomes Knoll Triple, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. again, with the Bonanza Carpet also ready at that time for the newest and youngest skiers & riders. 

We’re having a blast on the hill, come join us! 

– Evan

 

Period of 7 days/week lift operations: February 16th - March 2nd.  

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-26 05:57:33
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

The slopes have been skiing and riding amazingly, with packed powder all around the hill. We picked up an inch of fresh snow yesterday morning, adding a little extra sparkle and softness to the surface. 

We’re still 100% open, with consistent surfaces across the hill. Expect soft packed powder that holds an edge nicely and makes for smooth, confidence-inspiring turns. It’s classic skiing here, reliable, fun, and ready for another great day on snow.

A couple of light snow showers did pass through last night, leaving just a dusting of new stuff. Today starts mostly cloudy before the sun takes over. Temps top out near 18 at the top and 24 at the base with west winds around 17 to 22 mph making things feel a little chillier.

Our grooming team went after a similar plan, but they swapped out Knomes Knoll for Birch Glades, Summit Glades East and West run outs, and Sunset Schuss. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of groomed trails ahead of your day on the hill. Forty Niner will have great surfaces for smooth morning carving and Birch Glades should be covered in sweet machine groomed packed powder. 

The Summit Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. again, with the Bonanza Carpet also ready at that time for the newest and youngest skiers & riders. 

Get excited for Pico Race World to return to Lower Pike once again today. 

The hill is waiting, get out there! 

– Evan

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.  

Uphill Travel  

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open 

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.             

Mountain Services  

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close. 

Tickets & Lessons  

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation  

Pico Fitness Center                             

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.     

Hours of Operation:  

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m.
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.         

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-25 15:04:12
report:
Good morning, Pico family, 

Following up some warmer days, yesterday felt a little more like the colder days of late January, but it won’t last too long. By the end of today, we should be firmly back in the 20s and (hopefully) have some more snow to report! 

Snowfall should start sometime today and continue into the evening, with 1-3" projected during the day. Winds will feel similar to yesterday, but base temps will climb closer to 30° by the time lifts stop spinning. 

Despite the winds yesterday, surfaces stayed well intact, with groomers delivering a textbook carve-able surface. A Slope continues to deliver a fun groomed surface off the Little Pico Triple, while Lower Lift Line brings a fun challenge if you head over there from Swinger. With any luck, we’ll be getting a slight refresh from Mother Nature. 

The grooming plan was the same as the previous night’s, with Lower Sunset 71, Lower Giant Killer, and many more getting some corduroy. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of groomed trails ahead of your day on the hill. 

The Summit Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Golden Express Quad are scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. again, with the Bonanza Carpet also ready at that time for the newest and youngest skiers & riders. 

As a quick reminder, we’re open all week! If you’ve been waiting for a quieter midweek window to sneak in some turns, this is a great time to take advantage of a bonus Pico day. Looking slightly ahead, Pico Race World will return to Lower Pike once again this Thursday. 

Enjoy our final Pico Wednesday of the year! 

– Evan

  

Period of 7 days/week lift operations: February 16th - March 2nd.  

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open 

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.             

Mountain Services  

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close. 

Tickets & Lessons  

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation  

Pico Fitness Center                             

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.

Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.
